original: There’s a distant horn-blast, and you feel the first hints of a tremorous vibration in your [pc.feet]. You glance over your shoulder in time to see a vast steel behemoth roaring down the tracks towards Shineglass. It’s more than thirty cars long at a guess, all glittering silver with stark red lines painted down their sides. You can imagine the train was sleek and elegant looking once upon a time, but hard years and hard times have left an indelible mark: somebody’s hacked a huge turret onto the engine out of scrap steel, shielding a triple-barreled cannon that looks like it might as well have been ripped off the deck of an atomic-era Terran battleship. Armor plates have been riveted all along the flanks of the train cars, covering up most of the windows and wheels. The whole thing looks like a war-hulk more than a passenger liner, but you guess that’s the grim necessity of this war-ravaged world. The armored train barrels into the station, grinding to a halt with a screech of straining metal. Steam gutters out of vents along the engine car’s top, shrouding its turret and bathing you in the rank smell of diesel exhaust. By now the crowd that’d been milling around on the station platform has congealed into a solid press of bodies waiting to use the ticket machine. A cadre of humanoid robots disembark, stomping heavily onto the platform, each one standing protectively in front of one of the train’s scant few open doors. Elcine crosses her arms in front of herself and bows her head in reverence to the machines. “The blessed Evermind rewards our diligence, Emberian. Behold the holy DEE-SLs!” The nearest of the robots quirks its head when its model is invoked aloud. “Please present your ticket, prospective passenger.” Elcine squeals in excitement. “I never get tired of this. Here, holy one! One ticket - and first class, too!” The DEE-SL takes a step closer, swaying exaggeratedly large hips and a lengthy stabilizing tail as it comes over and takes the ticket from the priestess’s staff. It scans the ticket, nods, and returns it. “You may board, honored first class passenger. And you?” it asks, turning its red-eyed gaze towards you. You present your ticket for the same treatment. Satisfied, it nods its head and steps out of the way of the doors. “Thank you for choosing the Phaedran Railway Corporation, honored first class passenger. You have been assigned seats G1 and F1, in the scenic rear cabin.” The robot sweeps an arm towards the furthest car down the line, which does in fact have panoramic windows - though they’ve been partially armored over from the outside, it’s still a better view than anybody else on the train’s going to get. Elcine grins ear to ear and bounces onto the train, giving you and the rest of the gathered passengers a positively hypnotic view of jiggling cake as she mounts the stairs.
